phase: HACF Phase 2 Step A — Agent A4 (rules-builder, framework composition)
description: Cross-framework composition rules for how Next.js 15 + TypeScript + Tailwind 4 + Recharts 2.13 interact with FastAPI 0.115 + SQLAlchemy 2.x + Alembic 1.13 + pandas/numpy/scipy + pytest/Playwright for THIS specific project (live paper-trading + backtest on prediction markets). Individual per-framework rules live in rules-database-seed.json.

rules.rule: FastAPI's auto-generated OpenAPI schema at http://127.0.0.1:8000/openapi.json is the single authoritative contract between backend and frontend. Generate TypeScript types from it (e.g., via `openapi-typescript`) into `lib/api-types.ts` rather than hand-writing types that mirror pydantic models.
rules.rationale: Pydantic v2 BaseModel + FastAPI `response_model` produces a deterministic OpenAPI schema. Hand-written TS types drift; generated types guarantee the Market.yes_price bounds ([0,1]) and Platform enum stay in lockstep with backend.

rules.rule: Regenerate the TypeScript API types (`openapi-typescript http://127.0.0.1:8000/openapi.json -o lib/api-types.ts`) whenever any pydantic schema in `backend/app/schemas/` changes. Wire this into an npm script `types:gen` and call it as part of `predev` or on backend restart.
rules.rationale: Without regeneration on dev-server restart, frontend sees stale field names (e.g., renaming `yes_price` -> `yes`). Making the step explicit and scripted prevents silent UI breakage.

rules.rule: Zod schemas on the frontend must validate JSON returned from FastAPI at the network boundary (`MarketSchema.parse(await res.json())`) and must be kept structurally identical to the pydantic models. Any field divergence is a bug to fix on the pydantic side first, not in the zod schema.
rules.rationale: FastAPI is the source of truth; zod is a runtime guard against schema drift and upstream adapter corruption. Fixing divergence in zod instead of pydantic silently re-introduces the drift on the next backend restart.

rules.rule: Every Next.js `fetch()` call into the FastAPI backend that reads price/volume data must use `cache: 'no-store'` OR `next: { revalidate: 30 }` — never the default Next 15 behavior (uncached, but silently cached in some contexts). Pick `no-store` for dynamic market detail pages, `revalidate: 30` for market list pages (matches `live_poll_interval_seconds`).
rules.rationale: Next.js 15 made fetch uncached by default, but route handlers and server components still apply request memoization. Prices change every 30s; caching beyond the live_poll_interval serves stale prices and breaks bankroll math.

rules.rule: Client Components that display live prices must use SWR (or equivalent) with `refreshInterval: 30_000`, `refreshWhenHidden: false`, `refreshWhenOffline: false`. Server Components that render the initial HTML for the same page must fetch with `next: { revalidate: 30 }`. The two paths must agree on the 30-second cadence.
rules.rationale: Mismatched cadences cause UI flicker on hydration (SSR shows price X, client immediately refetches and shows Y). Recharts.md §4 explicitly recommends `refetchInterval: 30_000` to match `data_refresh_policy.live_poll_interval_seconds`.

rules.rule: All Recharts components must live under a `"use client"` module. Next.js page.tsx files (Server Components) may import them, but never render them in a Server Component path — instead, wrap in a thin Client Component that receives pre-fetched data as props.
rules.rationale: recharts.md §3 flags that Recharts uses browser SVG APIs and cannot render on the server. The pattern `<ServerPage><ClientChart data={...}/></ServerPage>` preserves SSR streaming while isolating the client boundary.

rules.rule: Every `ResponsiveContainer` in a Recharts chart must have a parent element with explicit height (either `h-[360px]`, `style={{ height: 360 }}`, or a flex/grid cell with defined height). When embedded in a Tailwind grid cell, set `grid-rows-[auto_1fr]` on the parent so the chart gets the flex slot.
rules.rationale: recharts.md §3 calls out the most common Recharts bug: chart doesn't render because parent has 0 height. Tailwind's default block-level h-auto leaves this ambiguous. A verifiable rule: chart container must have a computed height > 0 in DOM.

rules.rule: All Recharts `<Line>`, `<Area>`, and `<Bar>` components used for real-time (polled) series must set `isAnimationActive={false}`. Animation on 30-second repolls produces a visible stutter.
rules.rationale: recharts.md §3 pitfall list: 'isAnimationActive={false} for real-time data to avoid animation stutter on each update.' SWR's `mutate()` triggers React re-render on each poll; Recharts animates on every re-mount of data.

rules.rule: Tailwind v4 theme tokens declared in `app/globals.css` (inside `@theme { --color-yes, --color-no, --color-accent, ... }`) must also be mirrored in a TypeScript file `lib/chartTheme.ts` that Recharts reads for `stroke` and `fill` props. Never hard-code hex strings inside Recharts JSX.
rules.rationale: recharts.md §4 explicitly recommends 'Share a chartTheme.ts object with colors matching Tailwind tokens to avoid hard-coding hex strings.' Tailwind v4 CSS custom properties cannot be consumed by SVG `stroke` attributes directly without a helper.

rules.rule: FastAPI's `lifespan` asynccontextmanager is the single owner of the SQLAlchemy async engine. Never call `create_async_engine()` from a router, dependency, or background task — import the module-level engine from `backend/app/db.py` (or `main.py`).
rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md §1 and fastapi.md Example 1 show one engine per process, scoped to lifespan. Multiple engines exhaust SQLite's single-writer lock and Postgres connection pool.

rules.rule: Every route handler that touches the database must declare `session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)` and never create an `AsyncSession` directly. The `get_session` dependency must be an `async def` yielding from `async with SessionLocal() as session:` (not wrapped in `@asynccontextmanager`).
rules.rationale: fastapi.md §3 gotchas: 'DI with yield: use Depends(get_session) with AsyncIterator only; don't wrap in @asynccontextmanager.' Direct session creation bypasses FastAPI's exception-safe teardown.

rules.rule: SQLAlchemy sessions must never be shared across asyncio tasks, BackgroundTasks, or the polling loop. A background task that writes to DB must open its own `async with SessionLocal() as session:` block.
rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md §3: 'Don't share AsyncSession across tasks. One session per request/task.' FastAPI's `Depends(get_session)` closes the session after the HTTP response; a BackgroundTask running afterward would use a closed session.

rules.rule: All SQLAlchemy relationship traversals in async code must be eagerly loaded via `selectinload(...)` or `joinedload(...)` in the `.options()` clause of the query. Never access `market.ticks` after the session closes; the code must query `select(Market).options(selectinload(Market.ticks))` upfront.
rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md §3 gotchas: 'No implicit lazy loading in async. Accessing market.ticks after session close raises MissingGreenlet.' This is the #1 silent async SQLAlchemy bug.

rules.rule: `async_sessionmaker(..., expire_on_commit=False)` is mandatory for this project. Never use the default `expire_on_commit=True` — it forces a re-fetch of every attribute after commit, which in async code means re-opening the session.
rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md Example 1 + §3: 'expire_on_commit=False on session factory lets you access attributes after commit without refetch — recommended for web contexts.' Especially critical when returning the ORM object as `response_model`.

rules.rule: Every pydantic response model in `backend/app/schemas/` that is returned from a route with `response_model=...` must set `model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)`. Missing this flag means FastAPI cannot convert ORM instances and returns 500 errors at runtime.
rules.rationale: fastapi.md §3: 'from_attributes=True replaces v1's orm_mode = True.' Paired with `expire_on_commit=False`, this is the two-flag pattern that lets route handlers return ORM objects directly.

rules.rule: All Alembic migrations for this project must use `render_as_batch=True` in `env.py`'s `context.configure(...)` call, and any column alter / drop must wrap in `op.batch_alter_table(...)`. This is non-negotiable because the project ships with SQLite as the default database.
rules.rationale: alembic.md §2 and §4: 'SQLite cannot ALTER COLUMN — always wrap in op.batch_alter_table(...) / enable render_as_batch=True in env.py.' A migration that works on Postgres but not SQLite breaks the local dev loop.

rules.rule: Alembic `env.py` must use the async pattern: `async_engine_from_config(...)` + `await connection.run_sync(do_run_migrations)`. The sync pattern (`engine_from_config` + `connection.configure`) will raise `InvalidRequestError` with aiosqlite.
rules.rationale: alembic.md §2 and §4: 'Async env.py requires async_engine_from_config + connection.run_sync(do_run_migrations) — plain sync pattern won't work with aiosqlite.'

rules.rule: Alembic `target_metadata = Base.metadata` requires an import of `backend.app.models` (or whichever module actually defines the ORM classes) at the top of `env.py`. If models import lazily, autogenerate silently misses them.
rules.rationale: alembic.md §4: 'target_metadata = Base.metadata must import the module defining all models, or autogenerate will miss tables.' Verifiable: `alembic revision --autogenerate -m test` must list every ORM class.

rules.rule: Data migrations (UPDATE statements, backfills) must be separate Alembic revisions from schema migrations (CREATE/ALTER TABLE). Never mix `op.add_column(...)` and `conn.execute(sa.text('UPDATE ...'))` in the same revision unless the update is required to satisfy a NOT NULL constraint in the same migration — in which case it must use `op.batch_alter_table` to set the column non-nullable only after the update.
rules.rationale: alembic.md Example 3 shows the legitimate mix case. For all others, separating schema from data means you can roll back one without the other and the migration history reads as an audit log.

rules.rule: CI and pre-test setup scripts must run `alembic upgrade head` before executing pytest against a non-in-memory database. The in-memory test fixture (`sqlite+aiosqlite:///:memory:`) uses `Base.metadata.create_all` directly and does NOT go through Alembic — this is a deliberate speed choice but means migration correctness must be tested separately.
rules.rationale: alembic.md §4: 'CI: run alembic upgrade head before tests to ensure schema is current.' pytest.md Example 2 shows the in-memory fixture bypasses migrations. Both paths must exist.

rules.rule: Backend integration tests must use `httpx.AsyncClient(transport=ASGITransport(app=app))` against the full FastAPI app — not Starlette's `TestClient`. Use `@pytest.mark.asyncio` or project-wide `asyncio_mode = "auto"`.
rules.rationale: fastapi.md §4 and pytest.md Example 3: 'Use httpx.AsyncClient with ASGITransport (starlette's TestClient is sync and has edge cases with lifespan).' ASGITransport exercises the same code path (including lifespan) as uvicorn production.

rules.rule: The Playwright config's `webServer` array must launch BOTH the Next.js dev server (port 3000) and the FastAPI uvicorn server (port 8000). Both must be declared with their own health URL (`http://localhost:3000` and `http://localhost:8000/docs`) and `reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI`.
rules.rationale: vitest-playwright.md §3: 'Start both servers (Next.js + FastAPI) via webServer array — otherwise E2E tests hit a dead backend.' This is the dual-webServer pattern specific to full-stack Next+Python projects.

rules.rule: Playwright tests that verify backend error handling must use `page.route('**/api/**', route => route.fulfill({...}))` to intercept network calls, not hit a real backend with synthetic data. Tests that verify happy-path flows must hit the real FastAPI + SQLite webServer instance and seed state via `page.request.post('/api/...')` rather than `page.evaluate`.
rules.rationale: vitest-playwright.md §3 and Example 3: 'Avoid page.evaluate for test setup — prefer API calls or page.request to seed state.' Mocking only error paths avoids brittle tests on happy paths while keeping error paths deterministic.

rules.rule: Vitest cannot render Next.js Server Components. Any `page.tsx` or module using `await headers()`, `await cookies()`, or `await params` must either (a) extract its logic into a pure TS function tested separately, or (b) be tested via Playwright only. Do not try to mock React's Server Component runtime.
rules.rationale: vitest-playwright.md §3 Vitest gotchas: 'Server Components can't be rendered by Testing Library — they aren't real React components at runtime. Extract logic into pure functions or plain Client Components for unit testing.'

rules.rule: Pytest fixtures for async DB sessions must use `@pytest_asyncio.fixture` (not `@pytest.fixture`) even when `asyncio_mode = "auto"` is set in `pyproject.toml`. The auto-mode flag applies to test functions, not to fixtures.
rules.rationale: pytest.md §5 gotchas: 'asyncio_mode = "auto" removes the need to mark every async test; but fixtures still need @pytest_asyncio.fixture (not @pytest.fixture).' A `@pytest.fixture` on an async function silently becomes a coroutine object instead of its awaited value.

rules.rule: The backtest engine and the live paper-trading engine must share the same event-loop code path. The data-handler layer is polymorphic: `HistoricalDataHandler` replays tick rows from `price_ticks` table; `LiveDataHandler` pulls from the adapter polling loop. The strategy + portfolio + execution layers are identical across both modes.
rules.rationale: Architectural spec from semantic-spec.json and feature-discoveries.json: backtests must produce the exact same fills that live would, given identical price inputs. Divergent code paths introduce look-ahead bias and invalidate Sharpe/drawdown metrics.

rules.rule: Market data adapters (Kalshi, Polymarket, Manifold, PredictIt) must implement a common async interface `async def fetch_markets() -> list[NormalizedMarket]` and `async def fetch_ticks(market_id: str) -> list[NormalizedTick]`. The polling loop (a FastAPI `BackgroundTasks` task or an asyncio task started in `lifespan`) calls them on a 30-second interval and writes to `price_ticks`. Never call adapters directly from a route handler — always read from DB.
rules.rationale: tech-stack.json and fastapi.md Example 5: separation prevents blocking HTTP requests on upstream adapter latency. Routes read local SQLite (microseconds); the poller fills SQLite (seconds-scale network).

rules.rule: The polling loop for adapters must run inside the same FastAPI process as the API (started in `lifespan` via `asyncio.create_task(...)`), not as a separate worker or cron job. There is no queue (Celery/RQ/APScheduler) in the v1 stack — simplicity over durability.
rules.rationale: tech-stack.json deployment = local; fastapi.md §3: 'BackgroundTasks runs in the same worker after response — not durable. Use Celery/RQ/APScheduler for real queues.' For local paper-trading v1, the shared-process model is the simpler, correct choice.

rules.rule: Adapter responses must be normalized to the internal `NormalizedTick { ts: int_ms_utc, yes: float_0_1, no: float_0_1 }` shape at the adapter boundary. The discriminated-union pattern in typescript.md Example 6 has a Python mirror: each adapter module exports a `normalize(raw) -> NormalizedTick` function, and the polling loop calls only `normalize`.
rules.rationale: typescript.md Example 6: Kalshi returns `{yes_bid, yes_ask}`, Manifold returns `{prob}`. Polymarket returns `{outcomePrices: [yes, no]}`. Normalization at the boundary means the rest of the code (strategies, charts, DB) never branches on platform.

rules.rule: Strategy classes must implement the interface `class Strategy: def decide(self, market: Market, ticks: pd.Series, portfolio: Portfolio) -> Order | None; def on_fill(self, fill: Fill, portfolio: Portfolio) -> None`. Strategies live in `backend/app/strategies/*.py` and are auto-registered by filesystem scan at app startup via `lifespan`. Adding a new strategy file must NOT require editing a registry or restarting anything beyond `uvicorn --reload`.
rules.rationale: semantic-spec.json strategy plugin requirement: 'auto-register via filesystem scan; no server restart required on new plugin' under `uvicorn --reload`. pytest.md Example 1 shows the interface shape (kelly_fraction); the class form extends it with state for on_fill.

rules.rule: Strategy `decide()` must receive ticks as a `pd.Series` indexed by `DatetimeIndex` in UTC. Never pass raw tick dicts or NumPy arrays — the quant library (pandas-numpy-scipy.md) assumes DatetimeIndex for rolling windows, resample, and news-spike detection.
rules.rationale: pandas-numpy-scipy.md §3: 'Timezones: always construct DatetimeIndex with utc=True. Mixing naive and aware breaks joins.' Every metric (Sharpe, Sortino, max_drawdown, Brier) in that file assumes pd.Series input.

rules.rule: Quant functions (Sharpe, Sortino, max drawdown, Brier, Kelly) must never be called from inside an `async def` endpoint. Wrap in `await asyncio.to_thread(sharpe, returns)` or mount the endpoint as sync `def ...` so FastAPI runs it in a threadpool.
rules.rationale: fastapi.md §3 + pandas-numpy-scipy.md §4: pandas/numpy operations are CPU-bound and release the GIL only for specific NumPy ops. A blocking pandas call inside `async def` freezes all other concurrent requests (including the 30s polling loop).

rules.rule: SQLAlchemy `DateTime` columns must always be written as UTC, and every `datetime.utcnow` usage must be replaced with `datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)`. The pandas layer reads these timestamps via `pd.to_datetime(df['ts'], utc=True)`.
rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md Example 2 uses `datetime.utcnow` (a pitfall: returns naive); pandas-numpy-scipy.md §3 mandates `utc=True`. Mixing tz-aware (pandas) with tz-naive (SQLAlchemy default) raises `TypeError` on comparison.

rules.rule: The `price_ticks` table must have the composite index `(market_id, ts)` (named `ix_market_ts`). Every query that drives a chart or backtest does `WHERE market_id = ? ORDER BY ts` — without the composite index, SQLite falls back to a scan over all ticks across all markets.
rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md Example 2 explicitly declares `__table_args__ = (Index("ix_market_ts", "market_id", "ts"),)`. alembic.md Example 1 creates it. This is not optional for a 30-second polling workload over months.

rules.rule: Bulk tick inserts must use `session.execute(insert(PriceTick).values([...]))` with a list of dicts, NOT ORM-instance `session.add_all([...])`. For SQLite + aiosqlite, the bulk-statement path is 10x faster and avoids per-row identity-map overhead.
rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md Example 5 shows the bulk insert pattern; §3 notes 'SQLite + async serializes writes — do not expect real write concurrency.' Every unnecessary round-trip compounds under the single-writer lock.

rules.rule: Next.js route handlers that proxy the FastAPI backend (`app/api/backend/:path*`) must set `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` and forward the original request's `Accept` header. Use `next.config.ts` `rewrites()` only for static, header-less proxies; prefer explicit route handlers when any header forwarding is needed.
rules.rationale: nextjs.md Example 3 + §3: 'Route handlers need dynamic = "force-dynamic" when you read headers or use query params that change per request.' Example 6 shows the rewrite pattern, which is header-lossy.

rules.rule: FastAPI `CORSMiddleware` must be added BEFORE `app.include_router(...)` calls in `main.py`, and `allow_origins` must include exactly `http://localhost:3000` in dev. Wildcard `["*"]` is forbidden because the backend will eventually carry credentials for adapter API keys.
rules.rationale: fastapi.md Example 1 and §3: 'CORS middleware order: must be added before routers that return errors, else responses miss the CORS headers.' An error response from a router that lacks CORS headers appears as a network error in Next.js fetch.

rules.rule: Tailwind v4's `@theme` block is the single source of truth for colors AND spacing; do not install `tailwind.config.js`. Any v3-style config file must be deleted before running `pnpm dev`, or the `@tailwindcss/postcss` plugin will emit conflicting stylesheets.
rules.rationale: tailwind.md §4: 'Don't install tailwindcss@3 and v4 plugins side-by-side — the PostCSS plugin is a separate package in v4.' and 'No more content: [] array — delete the old config file when upgrading from v3.'

rules.rule: Zod schemas used for frontend form validation (e.g., backtest parameters: `{ strategy_id, start, end, bankroll }`) must match the FastAPI pydantic `CreateBacktestIn` exactly: same field names, same snake_case convention, same bounds (`bankroll: z.number().gt(0)` ↔ `Field(default=10_000, gt=0)`). Use one test that round-trips a valid object through both validators.
rules.rationale: typescript.md Example 2 + fastapi.md Example 2: zod and pydantic v2 have equivalent validator semantics (min/max/gt/ge), but field-casing mismatches silently drop values. A contract test prevents drift.

rules.rule: Server Actions in Next.js (`"use server"`) that POST to FastAPI must call `revalidatePath(...)` for the page that displays the resulting state. For backtests: POST `/api/backtests` then `revalidatePath('/backtests')`. Never `router.push(...)` inside a Server Action — use the form's redirect response or `revalidatePath` + `redirect`.
rules.rationale: nextjs.md Example 5 shows the pattern. Without `revalidatePath`, the user POSTs a backtest but the list page shows the stale pre-submission state until next poll.

rules.rule: The ORDER of hydration for a live market detail page is: (1) Server Component pre-fetches `/api/markets/{id}` with `cache: 'no-store'` and the last 100 ticks from `/api/markets/{id}/ticks?limit=100`; (2) passes pre-fetched data as props to a Client Component; (3) Client Component starts SWR with `refreshInterval: 30_000` using the pre-fetched data as `fallbackData`. This avoids the loading-spinner flash on first paint.
rules.rationale: nextjs.md Example 4 + recharts.md §4: Server Component SSR gives instant first paint; SWR's `fallbackData` option avoids re-fetching on mount. Both the 30s cadence and the 100-tick window match the project defaults in tech-stack.json.

rules.rule: Every new strategy plugin file dropped into `backend/app/strategies/` must carry a sibling test file in `tests/strategies/` that (a) constructs the strategy, (b) feeds it a deterministic `pd.Series` of ticks, (c) asserts a specific decision for at least one input. The strategy auto-registration scan must skip files whose tests do not exist (fail-closed).
rules.rationale: semantic-spec.json requires strategies to be verifiable; pytest.md Example 1 shows the pd.Series parametrize pattern. Fail-closed registration prevents half-built strategies from firing on live paper trading.
